entity.summary: Polygon-native decentralized exchange launched liquidity mining on 2020-10-09 and later expanded across multiple EVM networks and products. QuickSwap remained active after a May 2022 DNS/domain hijack redirected users through compromised infrastructure while core liquidity contracts and user wallets were reported safe.
entity.notes: Entity-level umbrella record covering the QuickSwap DEX brand rather than separate chain deployments or products such as QuickPerps and QuickSwap Lend.

events.title: QuickSwap launched liquidity mining on Polygon
events.description: QuickSwap launched liquidity mining on 2020-10-09 as a permissionless automated market maker using Polygon's low-cost EVM infrastructure, establishing the operating start date used by HEI.
events.notes: Official launch materials identify October 9, 2020 as the liquidity-mining and operational launch marker.

events.title: QuickSwap domain hijacked through registrar access
events.description: Attackers gained control of QuickSwap's DNS through the domain registrar and redirected the official site on 2022-05-14. QuickSwap regained control of the domain and reported that liquidity pools, staking contracts and user wallets remained safe, while some users interacting with the compromised frontend were affected.
events.notes: Classified as exploit because the incident compromised the official access layer and exposed users through a malicious frontend rather than exploiting the core AMM contracts.

evidence.title: Introducing QuickSwap: Meet the Next Generation of High-Performance DEX
evidence.notes: Primary project article stating that liquidity mining launched on Friday, 2020-10-09.

evidence.title: QUICK Tokenomics & Liquidity Mining Details
evidence.notes: Contemporaneous official launch and liquidity-mining source.

evidence.title: QuickSwap’s GoDaddy Domain Hijack: How it Happened & Our Proposal to Restore the Community
evidence.notes: Primary incident explanation describing registrar/DNS compromise, recovery and proposed compensation.

evidence.title: QuickSwap Has Been Domain Hijacked But Users' Funds Are Safe
evidence.notes: Contemporaneous independent report confirming the domain hijack and QuickSwap's statement that core user funds remained safe.

evidence.title: QuickSwap official blog
evidence.notes: Current official blog showing ongoing QuickSwap product and ecosystem activity through 2026.
